notes:
dài means long (in length). Use it for physical length and also for duration in some cases.

It’s an adjective and comes after the noun it describes.  

Use rất / quá / hơi before it to adjust intensity, and dài hơn for comparisons.  
Don’t confuse it with lâu (long time) — dài is about length, lâu is about time.

Opposite: ngắn (short).
exampleSentences: Tóc của chị ấy rất dài nên mỗi sáng chị phải chải lâu.
Con đường từ nhà anh đến trường khá dài và nhiều cây xanh.
Chiếc khăn này đủ dài để quấn quanh cổ hai lần.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Her hair is very long so she has to brush it for a long time every morning.
The road from his house to school is quite long and has many green trees.
This scarf is long enough to wrap around the neck twice.

definitions.en: Having a greater length than usual.
definitions.es: Tener una longitud mayor de lo habitual.
definitions.vi: Có chiều dài lớn hơn bình thường.

notes_md:
**dài** means **long** (in length). Use it for physical length and also for duration in some cases.

It’s an adjective and comes **after the noun** it describes.  

Use **rất / quá / hơi** before it to adjust intensity, and **dài hơn** for comparisons.  
Don’t confuse it with **lâu** (long time) — *dài* is about length, *lâu* is about time.

Opposite: **ngắn** (short).
